title: The Hogwarts Express
prose: Platform Nine and Three-Quarters is a wall like any other, until you walk straight at it and find a scarlet steam engine waiting on the other side, whistling importantly under a haze of steam, families crowded along the platform for last goodbyes.
You find a compartment already half-full of other first-years, all of you equally unsure where to look, trunks too big for the luggage racks wedged in every which way.

Somewhere around midday a trolley rattles down the corridor. "Anything off the trolley, dears?" Chocolate Frogs, Bertie Bott's Every Flavour Beans, Cauldron Cakes — you're regretting not asking for more spending money within about a minute.

The countryside rolls past for hours, farmland giving way to hills, hills to a loch reflecting the last of the daylight, before a voice crackles overhead: five minutes to Hogwarts. Leave your luggage on the train — it'll be taken up to the castle separately.

choices.label: Share your sweets around the compartment.
Hang back and watch the countryside go by.
